name: pcs-promo-parser
description: Parse vendor promo decks (Milwaukee, DeWalt, Makita, Bosch, EGO, Flex, GearWrench, Crescent) into PCS Kit Builder Stage 1 outputs. Handles kit promos, NLP price drops, RSAs, and exclusions (POS Redemption, ARP, brick-and-mortar, etc.). v1.4.0: robust + selective PDF ingestion — when the native PDF reader's rasteriser is missing (common in a Linux sandbox) it renders with PyMuPDF in-process (no poppler), and it skips cover/marketing/divider pages from the full-resolution vision read for big token savings with no accuracy loss; the Milwaukee SKU pattern now admits sub-brand forms (MX Fuel, letter-infix, bare models, Empire, Stiletto, apparel, layout codes) so legitimate SKUs stop being wrongly held to For-Review; ambiguous split battery slides ask the operator to confirm the tool-to-free-good pairing before holding. v1.3.0: a per-SKU + per-price verification gate runs before any output is written — every SKU/price is independently re-grounded in the deck (deterministic text grep + an independent adversarial verifier subagent + a vendor-regex check); anything ungroundable is HELD out of the Promo-List and surfaced in For-Review (Review Class `unverified`), so hallucinated SKUs/prices never reach NetSuite. v1.2.0: outputs are organized into `Parsed Decks/<Vendor>/<Vendor>-<QN>-<YYYY>-<MM-DD>/Promo Parsed Output/`; Buy-More-Save-More, e-rebate, and promo-code promos are now PARSED into a combined Other-Promotions.csv (no longer excluded); a conditional For-Review.xlsx collects low-confidence + missing-data pages with an in-chat table + link; empty outputs are no longer written, with the always-present Parser-Audit.csv as the run manifest. v1.1.0: split-slide per-group pairing, multiset `Get N` picks, Milwaukee on-slide pricing. v1.0.0: RSA-Kits/RSA-NLP outputs with credit amounts, Makita Needs-Pricing routing.
